topic: Lifestyle intervention explanation for a common chronic condition
reader: patient caregiver who needs a plain-language explanation with safety boundaries
use_context: A patient-safe medical explanation that avoids diagnosis, names red flags, and separates general education from personalized care.
required_claims: what the intervention can and cannot do
what should not be assumed from general evidence
prohibited_overclaims: does not diagnose the reader's condition

source_packets.title: Clinical guideline summary
source_packets.summary: Gives guideline-backed advice on first-line management, safety warnings, and escalation thresholds.

findings.statement: The intervention is recommended as part of a broader care plan rather than as a stand-alone fix.
findings.decision_note: Keep the explanation anchored in care planning, not miracle framing.
failure_modes: presenting the intervention as universally sufficient
misunderstandings: assuming the intervention replaces follow-up care

findings.statement: Certain warning signs require prompt clinician evaluation instead of home management.
findings.decision_note: This must be explicit in the final explanation.
failure_modes: leaving urgent symptoms buried in caveats
misunderstandings: treating all symptoms as safe to watch at home

source_packets.title: Peer-reviewed review article
source_packets.summary: Synthesizes effect sizes, adherence issues, and the limits of generalization across populations.

findings.statement: Average benefits vary substantially by baseline severity and adherence.
findings.decision_note: Use this to avoid one-size-fits-all language.
failure_modes: treating average outcomes as guaranteed outcomes
misunderstandings: assuming identical benefit for every reader

findings.statement: Non-medical lifestyle guidance is informative but cannot substitute for individualized care.
findings.decision_note: Keep the explanation from drifting into personal treatment advice.
failure_modes: presenting lifestyle advice as personalized therapy
misunderstandings: assuming general advice is enough for a diagnosis or treatment plan
